title: China’s New AI Is 6X More Efficient Than Claude
summary: Moonshot AI released Kimi K2.7 Code, a coding model for agents working on real codebases, while Z.ai released GLM-5.2 with a 1 million token context window for long-horizon software tasks. Both are open-weight models positioned as efficient alternatives to closed systems.
writeup: The video covers Moonshot AI's release of Kimi K2.7 Code, an open-weight coding model built for coding agents that operate across real codebases. It frames the model as efficient and competitive with closed coding systems, with the title citing a 6x efficiency edge over Claude. The takeaways: an open-weights option aimed at agentic coding work, positioned against proprietary alternatives on efficiency.
